steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question asking for the main topic of a short passage. The passage introduces a runestone discovered in Norway, gives its estimated carving date (1–250 CE), and notes what researchers hope to learn from it. The correct answer must cover the entire passage, not just one detail.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A runestone found in Norway: Every sentence in the passage relates to this discovery — its dating, its significance as the earliest written words in Scandinavia, and what researchers can learn from it. This covers the full scope of the text. ✓
  • Choice B — Battles of the Iron Age: The passage mentions the Iron Age only in the phrase "early Iron Age Scandinavia" as context for the runic alphabets. Battles are never discussed. ✗
  • Choice C — The research interests of Kristel Zilmer: Zilmer appears only in the final sentence as a named expert commenting on the runestone. Her research interests are not the passage's focus. ✗
  • Choice D — A new method for dating rock samples: Carbon dating is mentioned once as the method used to date the site. The passage does not present or evaluate a new dating method — it is simply cited as a tool. ✗
A is correct.
